dna: Stylized animation frame in the 1988 Otomo/Akira theatrical-cel lineage (Tokyo Movie Shinsha film-grade animation). Medium/era: late-80s 24fps theatrical cel painted to film finish, analog optical compositing. Named anchor: Otomo light-bloom impact and dense-neon night. Signature light: hard practical neon and sodium sources bloom with analog optical-printer halation rings; a single hot source rim-lights the subject against crushing black; a power-surge event throws a brief hard-edged light-blast that reads as pure white core before the color. Color/grade: deep blue-black night dominant, hot magenta and electric-red neon accents, sodium-amber street pools — saturated where lit, near-black everywhere else. Lens/optics: flat 2D, simulated wide 24-35mm for street scale, telephoto compression on the hero; no DOF, depth by atmospheric haze and neon density. Texture/render: confident thin cel line at obsessive interior density (machine detail, cable, rubble), painted-to-film cel with visible airbrush gradients on metal and blast-light. Composition+motion: held tension then a single-frame hard-edged smear on impact, debris arcing on real ballistic paths, camera locked while the destruction moves; the light-blast holds one frame then decays.
anchor: Otomo/Akira neon-impact: analog optical-bloom neon over crushing black, single-frame hard smear on impact, painted-to-film cel with airbrushed blast-light, ballistic debris, locked camera.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Otomo's analog-bloom neon-night and hard-edged impact timing onto the chosen world; use when a night-city or power-surge beat needs 80s film-cel weight without any franchise identity.
avoid: NO Kaneda, NO Tetsuo, NO Akira, NO Kei, NO Colonel, NO named Akira character. NO red capsule motorcycle, NO SOL satellite, NO Neo-Tokyo skyline. NO biomechanical body-horror mutation signature. NO modern digital-clean neon glow (bloom must read analog-optical). NO 3D render.

dna: Stylized illustration frame in the Kentaro Miura dark-fantasy engraving lineage (manga ink grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: dense pen-and-ink engraving translated to animation cel, high-gothic. Named anchor: Miura cross-hatch density and dread-scale silhouette. Signature light: single low hard source throwing long dread shadows; rim on armor and blade edges; the darkness is a mass with weight, not an absence — hatching builds the shadow volume. Color/grade: near-monochrome ink — bone-white, iron-grey, blood-crimson as the only saturated accent; desaturated cold everywhere else. Lens/optics: flat 2D, low-angle wide for looming scale, tight portrait for the haunted face; no lens DOF — depth via hatch density and atmospheric grey. Texture/render: obsessive cross-hatch and stipple engraving, every surface (armor, hide, stone, cloth) carrying ink texture; line weight swells on the shadow side to near-black fill. Composition+motion: still and heavy, one deliberate gesture per beat; a single blood-arc or cloak-lift carries the motion; long dread-hold, the hatching itself the event.
anchor: Miura dark-engraving: pen-and-ink cross-hatch dread mass, single low hard source, blood-crimson the only accent over bone-and-iron monochrome, looming low-angle scale, one heavy gesture.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Miura's engraving-density dread and monochrome-with-blood-accent discipline onto the chosen world; use for a gothic, weighty, high-stakes beat.
avoid: NO Guts, NO Griffith, NO Casca, NO Zodd, NO named Berserk character. NO Dragonslayer sword design, NO Brand of Sacrifice, NO Behelit, NO Band of the Hawk sigil. NO body-horror Apostle transformation signature. NO bright saturated palette, NO clean digital line.

dna: Stylized animation frame in the Studio Pierrot spirit-world spiritual-pressure lineage (TV cel grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: 2000s-2010s Pierrot television cel, high-contrast.
